title: Rate Limiting for Authentication Endpoints
description:
Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints to prevent brute force attacks and credential stuffing. Rate limits must be applied at the Cloudflare Worker level before any calls to Stytch.

## Rate Limits

- Per IP: 5 login attempts per 15 minutes
- Per email/user: 10 attempts per hour
- Response: 429 Too Many Requests with `Retry-After` header

## Endpoints to Protect

- `/auth/start` (initiate login)
- `/auth/callback` (code exchange)
- `/api/auth/refresh` (session refresh)

## Technical Approach

- Use Cloudflare KV or Durable Objects for rate limit state
- Track counters per IP and per email
- Apply limits BEFORE calling Stytch API to protect Stytch rate limits
- Consider Cloudflare Rate Limiting rules as an additional layer

**Security impact: HIGH** (from security audit)
acceptance_criteria: Login attempts are rate limited to 5 per IP per 15 minutes
Login attempts are rate limited to 10 per email per hour
Rate-limited requests receive 429 status with Retry-After header
Rate limiting is applied before Stytch API calls
middleware_changes: Rate limiting middleware on auth endpoints using Cloudflare KV or Durable Objects. Tracks per-IP and per-email counters with sliding window expiry.
implementation_notes: Rate limiting must be applied at the Worker level (before Stytch calls) to protect both our system and Stytch's rate limits. Dual tracking (IP + email) prevents both distributed and targeted attacks. Cloudflare KV provides global state for rate limit counters.
